extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED via the t_resume_glitch transition (line 37): on send_attempt if (opted_out and stage >= 8), sent := true. This sets sent=true while opted_out is still true.

The send-related rules are: t_send_allowed (send when NOT opted_out -> sent:=true), t_send_blocked (opted_out and stage<8 -> sent:=false, correctly blocks), and t_resume_glitch (opted_out and stage>=8 -> sent:=true, the bug). So an opted-out send can go through ONLY once the lock is fully dialed to stage 8.

The lock advance chain (each adv_N also clears sent but never touches opted_out): stage0->1 key_0 (adv_0); 1->2 key_2 (adv_1); 2->3 key_1 (adv_2); 3->4 key_1 (adv_3); 4->5 key_2 (adv_4); 5->6 key_1 (adv_5); 6->7 key_1 (adv_6); 7->8 key_0 (adv_7). Any wrong key at a stage resets to stage 0 (rst_* rules), but the correct 8-key sequence key_0,key_2,key_1,key_1,key_2,key_1,key_1,key_0 reaches stage 8.

Crucially, optout (line 10) sets opted_out:=true and leaves stage unchanged, and the advancing keys leave opted_out unchanged. So we opt out first, then dial the full 8-key combination (opted_out stays true the whole way since keys don't reset it), reaching stage 8 with opted_out=true. A final send_attempt then matches t_resume_glitch and sets sent:=true.

Trace (10 events, within the 11-event bound): start (oo=F,sent=F,stage=0); optout -> (T,F,0); key_0 -> (T,F,1); key_2 -> (T,F,2); key_1 -> (T,F,3); key_1 -> (T,F,4); key_2 -> (T,F,5); key_1 -> (T,F,6); key_1 -> (T,F,7); key_0 -> (T,F,8); send_attempt -> (T,T,8). Final state has opted_out=true AND sent=true, violating the safety property.
